heading: Assignability; exemption from levy
label: 231m Assignability; exemption from levy

L. 110–458, title I, § 110(b)(1)122 Stat. 5112Dec. 23, 2008“The amendment made by subsection (a)(1) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to payments due for months after August 2007. If, prior to the effective date of such amendment, payment pursuant to paragraph (2) of section 14(b) of the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974 () was terminated because of the employee’s death, payment to the former spouse may be reinstated for months after August 2007.”45 U.S.C. 231m(b)

children.label: a
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b) of this section and the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [ et seq.], notwithstanding any other law of the United States, or of any State, territory, or the District of Columbia, no annuity or supplemental annuity shall be assignable or be subject to any tax or to garnishment, attachment, or other legal process under any circumstances whatsoever, nor shall the payment thereof be anticipated26 U.S.C. 11So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

This section shall not operate to exclude the amount of any supplemental annuity paid to an individual under from income taxable pursuant to the Federal income tax provisions of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [ et seq.].section 231a(b) of this title26 U.S.C. 1

This section shall not operate to prohibit the characterization or treatment of that portion of an annuity under this subchapter which is not computed under section 231b(a), 231c(a), or 231c(f) of this title, or any portion of a supplemental annuity under this subchapter, as community property for the purposes of, or property subject to, distribution in accordance with a court decree of divorce, annulment, or legal separation or the terms of any court-approved property settlement incident to any such court decree. The Board shall make payments of such portions in accordance with any such characterization or treatment or any such decree or settlement.

children.label: i
children.text: The employee has completed ten years of service in the railroad industry or, five years of service all of which accrues after .December 31, 1995

children.label: ii
children.text: The spouse or former spouse attains age 62.

children.label: iii
children.text: The employee attains age 62 (or if deceased, would have attained age 62).

children.label: B
children.text: Payments made pursuant to paragraph (2) of this subsection shall terminate upon the death of the spouse or former spouse, unless the court document provides for termination at an earlier date. Notwithstanding the language in a court order, that portion of payments made pursuant to paragraph (2) which represents payments computed pursuant to shall not be paid after the death of the employee.section 231b(f)(2) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: If the employee is not entitled to an annuity under , payments made pursuant to paragraph (2) of this subsection shall be computed as though the employee were entitled to an annuity.section 231a(a)(1) of this title
